thesis: LOW-PE-attention vertical. Pop-A-Lock excluded (franchise). No national PE platform actively rolling up TX commercial locksmiths. The off-market thesis is that ETA / search-fund / independent-sponsor buyers face dramatically less bid competition than for dental / HVAC / plumbing.

sources_worked.note: direct WebFetch on company homepages and about pages — primary verification
WebSearch results for legal names, founder identification, family ownership, license #s
BBB profiles surfaced owner/principal names not exposed on company websites (AAA Locksmith Scott White, Texas Safe & Lock Hallford/Stogner, Whitley's Gaither)
LinkedIn + ZoomInfo profiles for cross-verification — critical for Dixie Safe & Lock second-gen Joseph Rushing discovery; Alert Lock Hitchcock multi-family identification
Yelp business profiles for review-count + Google reviews velocity proxy

sources_partial.issue: Most TX locksmith shops have THIN team pages. ~60% of attempts returned 404 on common about-page paths. Owner / leadership names not exposed on public sites for most candidates. This is a structural limitation of the locksmith vertical (locksmiths are notoriously bad at web marketing per the skill thesis) and limits the live-fetch successor-check to BBB/LinkedIn cross-references.

a_tier_deep_dive.narrative: Initial scoring produced 1 provisional A_acquire_self (Dixie Safe & Lock, final 77). Deep-dive on the top 3 boundary candidates revealed: (1) Dixie Safe & Lock — LinkedIn cross-check surfaced JOSEPH RUSHING (same surname as founder Harry Rushing) as Buyer / Inventory Control at the company — second-gen successor candidate present. Demoted A→B per skill verifying-no-successor protocol. (2) AAA Locksmith — BBB cross-check revealed SCOTT WHITE (same surname as founder Lloyd H. White) as current Manager — clean second-gen handoff. Demoted B→C. (3) Alert Lock & Key — LinkedIn + ZoomInfo cross-check surfaced THREE Hitchcock family members (Jerry, Freda, Justin) active across two generations. Demoted B→C. Net: 0 A-tier final candidates. Bilco Lock & Safe + Ace Security Solutions remain the strongest B-tier candidates at the A-tier boundary (final 76 each); CAD OV65 + voter DOB confirmation in next run could promote either to A.

supabase_write.reason: All 37 businesses + 37 business_scores rows upserted to offmarket schema. score_runs.business_count updated to 37. Verified via SELECT final_tier, count(*) FROM scored_targets WHERE score_run_id = '0690a07f-dee6-42aa-af23-3cf2257ad6cd' returning 0/10/22/5 (A/B/C/D).
supabase_write.business_signals_skipped: business_signals rows were NOT separately persisted in this run — signal-level evidence is embedded in the businesses.raw_enrichment.signals jsonb (visible in local locksmith_targets.json) and in the layer comments on business_scores. Future iteration could decompose into business_signals rows for richer query patterns.
